name: wormhole
description: Evidence-gated planning and orchestration state for AI coding agents.
long_description: Wormhole exposes a local MCP server for evidence records, open-question gates, orchestration task state, external agent dispatch, typed artifacts, and workbench rendering.

tools.name: round_start
tools.description: Start a gather and reason round.

tools.name: record_question
tools.description: Record an open question or assumption gap.

tools.name: gate_request
tools.description: Evaluate whether the mission can emit its final plan.

tools.name: agent_register
tools.description: Register an external AI agent or provider worker.

tools.name: agent_dispatch
tools.description: Dispatch a task to a registered external agent.

tools.name: agent_interrupt
tools.description: Interrupt an external agent run when supported.

tools.name: printing_press_register
tools.description: Register a Printing Press generated CLI.

tools.name: printing_press_select
tools.description: Select a Printing Press CLI by capability.

tools.name: printing_press_register_agent
tools.description: Convert a Printing Press CLI into a Wormhole external agent.

prompts.name: wormhole_orchestrate
prompts.description: Run a structured Wormhole planning or orchestration mission.
prompts.text:
Use Wormhole for this mission: ${arguments.objective}

Follow the Wormhole loop strictly: mission_start, round_start, gather evidence, record_evidence, record_question for gaps, register tasks or external agents when parallel work is useful, gate_request, then emit_plan only after the gate opens. Use printing_press_register and printing_press_register_agent for generated CLIs, then agent_dispatch only when their capabilities match the task.
